name: Authentication Bypass: OpenSSL CTX Object Modified after SSL Objects are Created
description: The product modifies an OpenSSL SSL_CTX object after SSL objects have already been created from it. Changes to the context can retroactively affect existing SSL objects, potentially weakening or bypassing encryption and authentication protections.
detection_pattern: EXTERNAL(SSL_CTX) node is modified by a TRANSFORM node after EXTERNAL(SSL) objects have been instantiated from it. The temporal ordering shows a CONTROL flow where context modification occurs after object creation, without re-initialization of dependent SSL objects.
